programme.desc: Donna gets tickets to the Packers game, and Eric convinces her to give one to Red, but he regrets his decision after Red's behavior gets them thrown out of the game.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: George Carlin's comedy bit "Seven Dirty Words" is the inspiration for a revenge plot when Donna is fired from the radio station for refusing to wear a bikini.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Eric inspires Jackie to take control of her life, after she freezes on camera during her own public access show.

programme.desc: When Eric decides to go on the road to discover life outside Point Place, Leo (Tommy Chong) comes to his rescue.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.sub-title: Oh Baby (We Got a Good Thing Goin')

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.sub-title: What You See Is What You Get
programme.desc: Tim's (Tim Allen) obvious enjoyment of some breast-enlargement literature leaves Jill (Patricia Richardson) feeling undesirable.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Randy, pretending to be thirty-two years old, meets a woman on a singles message board on the computer, and she comes by for a visit; Tim tries to help Mark with his science project.

programme.title: Bob Hearts Abishola
programme.desc: Abishola must draw the line when Bob's ex-wife tries to maneuver her way back into Bob's life; Goodwin is concerned when Christina sets her romantic sights on Kofo.

programme.title: Bob Hearts Abishola
programme.desc: Dottie makes an unexpected connection when she meets a fellow stroke survivor at physical therapy; Bob and Douglas try to keep tabs on Christina while she's away at an emotional wellness spa.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Raging Jim accidentally backs into a police car while chasing a guy and chooses anger-management classes instead of paying the fine.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim takes over housekeeping duties to give Cheryl time to grieve when her favorite uncle dies, but begins to suspect Cheryl is faking her grief to get him to do more housework.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike and Chuck's plans to open a classic car renovation service crash when Joe is invited to join in; Vanessa realizes that Mandy is tricking Kyle.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: After a chat with Bonnie, Mike struggles to balance his need for "me time" and finding common hobbies with Vanessa.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: When Kristin entrusts Mike with her gender reveal party, he promptly holds it over Vanessa's head; Chuck and Joe regret turning Ed's spare room into an oversized dollhouse for Bonnie.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Vanessa is secretly obsessed with Jen's new video game.

programme.desc: Adam and Andi leave Kate in charge at home for the first time after their baby sitter cancels on Valentine's Day; Don forgets to make romantic plans for Marcy.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Cheryl urges Jim to behave on St. Patrick's Day and forgo his annual law-bending "Green Man" routine so she can assure herself an appointment to the church committee.

programme.desc: Andy starts to play chess with Ryan on a regular basis, and jealous Jim assumes Andy is no longer his best friend.

programme.title: According to Jim

programme.desc: After Tim (Tim Allen) criticizes Jill (Patricia Richardson) for the car getting scratched, he nearly totals it himself.

programme.desc: Tim is less than thrilled about turning 40 when his friend has a heart attack.

programme.desc: The man who first recognized Tim's need for "more power" pays his former high-school student a visit.

programme.desc: Jill (Patricia Richardson) almost spoils Tim's (Tim Allen) chance at getting his hot rod featured in a major magazine.

programme.title: Nature
programme.sub-title: Raptors: A Fistful of Daggers: Meet the Raptors
programme.desc: The many species of raptors, from giant eagles to miniature falconets, and how they survive bitter winters, learn to hunt and undergo epic migrations.

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: The investigation into the March 2024 container ship collision that resulted in the collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore.

programme.title: Secrets of the Dead
programme.desc: Archaeological evidence suggests that the legend of King Arthur began in a 5th-century trading village following the departure of the Romans.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis

programme.title: Rick Renner
programme.sub-title: The Horror of a Roman Scourging
programme.desc: Rick Renner explains what it is like for a person to be scourged and shares what it is to be healed by the stripes of Jesus.

programme.title: You'll Get Through This
programme.sub-title: Is God Good When Life Isn't?
programme.desc: Max Lucado discusses trusting God.

programme.title: Joyce Meyer Enjoying Everyday Life
programme.sub-title: Healing the Soul of a Woman
programme.desc: Strong women get healed and restored and keep on reaching for their dreams. Learn how to grow strong despite the hurts you've been through!

programme.title: Turning Point With Dr. David Jeremiah
programme.sub-title: The Seven Churches of Revelation: The Faithful Church
programme.desc: The church in Philadelphia is commended for three principles that can be lost among the practices and programs of the modern church.

programme.title: Stakelbeck Tonight
programme.desc: Host Erick Stakelbeck takes viewers around the world; from Jerusalem to London to Washington, D.C.; pointing beyond with cutting-edge commentary, analysis and interviews focusing on ever-changing events and why they matter.

programme.title: Joel Osteen
programme.desc: Joel and Victoria Osteen's message of unconditional love and unending hope reaches everyone across the world.

programme.title: Main Stage
programme.desc: Charles Billingsley and TaRanda Greene; host Jason Crabb.

programme.title: Jesus: The Game Changer
programme.desc: Karl Faase travels to the U.K., U.S., Singapore, India and Australia, interviewing authors, academics and modern-day game changers about how the life and teaching of Jesus changed the world and why it matters.

programme.title: Insights -- Israel and the Middle East
programme.desc: Exploring the discoveries that support the Bible and help to understand the past.

programme.title: Signs & Secrets of the Messiah with Rabbi Jason Sobel
programme.sub-title: The Signs and Secrets of Healing (Part 2)
programme.desc: Rabbi Jason continues to explore the healing at the pool of Bethesda; diving into Yeshua's radical move to heal the man on a day of rest and how all of this connects to an Old Testament healing.

programme.title: Praise
programme.desc: Sheila Walsh hosts Darren Wilson from Los Angeles.

programme.title: In Touch With Dr. Charles Stanley
programme.desc: Charles Stanley, pastor of the First Baptist Church in Atlanta, shares his insights on Christian faith and enlightens viewers with spiritual motivation.
